one_liner: AAPL +4.84% to $308 on foldable iPhone production reports (10M units late 2026–mid 2027) adding $182B market cap — new product category catalyst, but DCF still 59% overvalued at base case.

news_catalyst.headline: Apple preparing ~10M foldable iPhone production starting late 2026, adding $182B market cap
news_catalyst.summary: Reports that Apple is ramping foldable iPhone production to ~10M units between late 2026 and mid-2027. This enters a premium device category where Samsung leads. Foldable iPhones command $1,800+ price points vs standard iPhone pricing, which would meaningfully lift Apple ASP and gross margins. The move also offsets the Chinese chipmaker CXMT/YMTC headline risk earlier this week. The 1.21x volume on the gap-up and hold-near-highs is institutional rather than retail-panic buying.

technical.summary: AAPL's post-June 26 Buying Climax (4.42x, 261M shares) structure shows the $273-274 floor holding. Today's news-driven gap from $289 to $308 on 1.21x volume is not climactic — it reads as a genuine Sign of Strength (SOS) impulse from the base. Close near the high of day at $308.63 vs $309.42 high is the ideal candle. Support: today's open at $294. Resistance: $309-315. DCF base $194.2 requires bull case ($333+) to justify current price — foldable iPhone premium ASP is what the market is pricing in.

wyckoff_narrative: AAPL is in post-SC absorption transitioning toward a potential Sign of Strength. The June 26 SC (4.42x volume) was the structural low; today's 1.21x advance to $308.63 on a clear catalyst is consistent with early Phase D markup. Confirmation: hold $294 open level on any near-term consolidation, then advance through $315+ on similar volume.

valuation.summary: DCF base $194.2 vs $308.63 = 59% overvalued. Bull DCF ~$333 is within reach if foldable iPhone drives meaningful ASP expansion. Not a value play; a growth narrative play.

risks: 59% above DCF base — foldable iPhone must succeed to justify valuation
CXMT/YMTC geopolitical escalation remains an unresolved supply chain overhang
Today's close at $308 approaches prior $287-288 distribution zone — overhead supply exists
